chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Const. Petition No.D-62 of 2024 Date Order with signature of Judge For hearing of CMA No.398/2024 27-02-2024 Mr. Mujahid Ali Shah, Advocate for Petitioner Nisar Ahmed Shaikh, Registrar NBA Court-1 Amanullah Shaikh, Registrar NAB Court-III.-.-.-. -.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that although petitioner Mst. Parvezan was granted protective bail on 16.01.2024 for eight days in Reference No.6/2016 ‘Re-State v/s Muhammad Arif Teevno and others’, but since in the order the observation regarding suspension of NBWs is not mentioned, hence when he appeared before the Court, an observation was made by learned Judge of the NAB Court that until and unless the NBWs are suspended, he will take petitioner in custody irrespective of protective bail. However the Registrars who after notice of this application are present have denied such fact and submit that after filing of the application on 01.02.2024 learned counsel withdrew the same before it was presented in the Court. We therefore find no merits in the application u/s 151 CPC, and dismissed it accordingly, however, allow the petitioner to appear before the NAB Court by tomorrow without fail in terms of order dated 16.01.2024. Till tomorrow the NBWs against her shall remain suspended. This order shall ceased to have its effect after expiry of above mentioned period or when the petitioner surrenders before the Court concerned which one occurs earlier. It is however, made clear that in case petitioner fails to surrender before the Court concerned within stipulated period, her bail bonds shall stand automatically forfeited.

This order shall ceased to have its effect after expiry of above mentioned period or when the petitioner surrenders before the Court concerned which one occurs earlier. It is however, made clear that in case petitioner fails to surrender before the Court concerned within stipulated period, her bail bonds shall stand automatically forfeited. The listed application stands disposed of in the above terms. JUDGE JUDGE Sulemen Khan/PA
